product_overview: Multi-tenant SaaS ISP billing backend that manages users, tenants, clients and routers, integrates RADIUS accounting (radacct) from MikroTik RouterOS, and enforces tenant isolation. The PRD notes core tables (users, radcheck, radacct, plans, sessions) and the need to ensure each tenant only accesses their own data (see PRD page 1).
core_goals: Enforce strict tenant isolation across all read/write operations
Provide secure user authentication (JWT) and per-tenant profile access
Expose tenant-scoped CRUD for ISP clients and routers
Ingest and surface RADIUS accounting/session data (radacct) from MikroTik via FreeRADIUS
Provide clear error modes and validation to prevent cross-tenant data leakage
Ship a migration and schema design that adds tenant_id to all relevant tables

features.name: User Authentication API
features.description: Register new tenants/users, authenticate with JWT, and return tenant-scoped profile (endpoints under src/app/api/auth). All auth flows must produce a token that includes tenant_id for downstream tenant enforcement.
user_flows: POST /api/auth/register with valid {email,password,tenantName} -> Receive 201 with created User and Tenant object (includes tenant_id) -> POST /api/auth/login with email/password -> Receive 200 with JWT token and user object (token contains tenant_id) -> GET /api/auth/me with Authorization: Bearer <token> -> Receive 200 with user profile (tenant_id present)
POST /api/auth/register with existing email -> Receive 409 Conflict error
POST /api/auth/login with wrong password -> Receive 401 Invalid credentials
GET /api/auth/me with expired/invalid token -> Receive 401 Unauthorized

features.name: Client Management API
features.description: Create and list ISP clients scoped to the authenticated user's tenant (endpoints in src/app/api/clients). Backend must ignore any client-supplied tenant_id and instead associate client with tenant_id from JWT.
user_flows: POST /api/auth/login with valid credentials -> Receive 200 with JWT token -> POST /api/clients with Authorization: Bearer <token> and body {fullName,phone,planId} -> Receive 201 with created client object (client.tenant_id == token.tenant_id) -> GET /api/clients with Authorization: Bearer <token> -> Receive 200 with array of clients filtered to token.tenant_id
POST /api/clients without Authorization header -> Receive 401 Unauthorized
POST /api/clients with invalid/missing planId -> Receive 400 Bad Request (validation error)

POST /api/clients with a body that includes tenant_id of another tenant -> Backend ignores provided tenant_id and returns 201 with created client assigned to token.tenant_id (or returns 403 if a policy forbids overriding tenant)

features.name: Router Management API
features.description: Add and list routers for a tenant so routers can be configured to send RADIUS accounting to the system. Routers are tenant-scoped and must be associated with the user's tenant. (endpoints in src/app/api/routers).
user_flows: POST /api/auth/login with valid credentials -> Receive 200 with JWT token -> POST /api/routers with Authorization: Bearer <token> and body {ip,sharedSecret,hostname,accountingEnabled:true} -> Receive 201 with created router object (router.tenant_id == token.tenant_id) -> GET /api/routers with Authorization: Bearer <token> -> Receive 200 with array of routers for token.tenant_id
POST /api/routers with missing required fields (e.g., sharedSecret) -> Receive 400 Bad Request

POST /api/routers with accountingEnabled:true but router not reachable -> Receive 201 router created; subsequent monitoring/status endpoint (or GET /api/routers) should show unreachable/No accounting sessions (business-level error)

features.name: Tenant Isolation Enforcement
features.description: Middleware and request-flow patterns that enforce tenant isolation using tenant_id embedded in JWT. Every protected endpoint must derive tenant_id from the token and apply it to DB queries (no trust in request body/query).
user_flows: POST /api/auth/login -> Receive 200 with JWT token (token includes tenant_id) -> GET /api/clients with Authorization: Bearer <token> -> Backend uses token.tenant_id in DB query (e.g., WHERE tenant_id = token.tenant_id) -> Receive 200 with clients only for that tenant (happy path)

POST /api/clients with Authorization: Bearer <token> and body containing tenant_id of a different tenant -> Backend rejects or ignores supplied tenant_id and either returns 403 Forbidden or returns 201 with created client assigned to token.tenant_id (error flow demonstrates enforcement)
GET /api/clients with a token belonging to tenant A but tampered to reference tenant B -> Token verification fails or tenant mismatch results in 401/403 -> Receive 401/403 (error path)
GET /api/routers with Authorization missing tenant claim (malformed token) -> Receive 401 Unauthorized

features.name: RADIUS Accounting & Router Sync
features.description: Flows that onboard a router (MikroTik) and populate radacct/session accounting via FreeRADIUS. The system expects radacct rows in the DB linked to client user accounts and tenant_id. API-level actions facilitate adding routers and verifying accounting data visibility per tenant.
user_flows: POST /api/auth/login -> Receive 200 with JWT token -> POST /api/routers with Authorization: Bearer <token> and body {ip,sharedSecret,accountingPort,hotspotEnabled:true,pppoeEnabled:true} -> Receive 201 with router object -> (external) Configure MikroTik with RouterOS RADIUS settings to send accounting to FreeRADIUS (out-of-band) -> FreeRADIUS writes radacct rows into shared DB (radacct rows must include username and tenant_id mapping via radcheck/user join) -> GET /api/clients with Authorization: Bearer <token> -> Receive 200 and clients now include session/accounting metadata from radacct (sessions filtered to token.tenant_id)

POST /api/routers with incorrect sharedSecret or missing accounting config -> Receive 201 (router created) but subsequent GET /api/clients shows no accounting/session entries for that router (operational error)
POST /api/routers with malformed payload -> Receive 400 Bad Request

features.name: User Authentication API
features.description: User registration, login, and profile management

endpoints.description: Register a new user and tenant
List clients for the current tenant
List routers for the current tenant
